extracted_jd.hiring_manager_name: Alex Chen, Head of Platform Engineering
extracted_jd.jd_text_full: We are looking for a Senior Software Engineer to join our Platform Engineering team, reporting to Alex Chen. You will design and build distributed systems handling 1M+ RPS. Requirements: 5+ years Python, strong AWS and Kubernetes experience, PostgreSQL at scale. We run blameless postmortems and have a no-on-call-unless-you-own-it policy.
specificity_signals: reporting to Alex Chen, Head of Platform Engineering

scraped_pages.title: Careers at Acme Tech
scraped_pages.text:
Careers at Acme Tech

We build infrastructure for the next generation of fintech. Our engineering team ships autonomously. We value blameless postmortems and psychological safety. We hire for craft and curiosity.

What we offer
- Salary band £70,000 - £90,000 for Senior Software Engineers in London
- Equity grant on a four-year vest with a one-year cliff
- Hybrid working: minimum two days per week in our Shoreditch office, otherwise wherever is most productive for you
- 28 days annual leave plus public holidays
- Private medical with Vitality, contribution to a workplace pension at 6% matched
- Visa sponsorship for the Skilled Worker route, including for new entrants on the Graduate visa

How we work
- We do not have an on-call rota for engineers who do not own the service. If you build it, you carry the pager. If you do not, you sleep.
- Every incident produces a blameless postmortem within five working days. The postmortem document is shared company-wide and discussed in the next engineering all-hands.
- We deploy on Fridays. We trust our test coverage and our rollout tooling. If you cannot deploy on a Friday it is the build pipeline that is broken, not the calendar.
- Code review is required before merge. We aim for first response within two hours during working hours.

What we look for
- Five or more years building production distributed systems
- Strong Python, comfortable in Go or Rust
- Direct experience operating Kubernetes in production, not just authoring manifests
- A bias for shipping small changes frequently rather than large changes occasionally
- Calm written communication. Most of our coordination happens in writing across timezones.

Apply by emailing careers@acmetech.io with a CV and a paragraph on a system you shipped end-to-end.

scraped_pages.title: Engineering Blog
scraped_pages.text:
How we eliminated 400ms of p99 latency in our payment processing pipeline

Last quarter our payment processing pipeline was the slowest service in production. p50 sat comfortably under 80ms but p99 stretched past 600ms during peak. Customer complaints rose. We set ourselves a target: cut p99 to under 200ms within one quarter.

We did three things, in order.

First, we replaced our serial validator chain with a parallel fan-out. The chain was historical: each validator ran sequentially because the second one used to depend on the first one's enriched output. That dependency stopped being true in 2023 but the chain stayed. Switching to asyncio.gather across the four independent validators bought us back 180ms at p99.

Second, we moved our currency conversion lookup from a per-request database query to an in-memory LRU cache populated from Postgres on service start, with a five-minute background refresh. This cost us a small amount of staleness on FX moves and saved a synchronous DB hop on every payment. Another 140ms at p99 vanished.

Third, and this is the boring one, we tuned our connection pool. We had been running with 20 max connections per pod against a database that comfortably handles 400. Bumping the pool to 60 cut connection-wait time during burst traffic and shaved the last 80ms.

Net result: p99 went from 615ms to 195ms. Customer complaints dropped to zero in the following month. The team of 45 engineers in our payments group ships twice a week and we now have headroom to scale into the next traffic tier.

If working on this kind of problem appeals, we are hiring senior engineers in London. See acmetech.io/careers for the live posting.

culture_claims.claim: Engineers ship autonomously with no micro-management

culture_claims.verbatim_snippet: We value blameless postmortems and psychological safety.
